Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.fifa.com
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For a YES, Bolivia must win the specific FIFA-listed match on 2025-10-14 within 90 minutes plus stoppage time. That requires (1) the match actually being played as scheduled or after postponement, and (2) Bolivia outperforming its opponent in a competitive CONMEBOL context. Base rate: Bolivia historically has a below-average international win rate, especially against South American opposition, and even with home/altitude advantages it is usually an underdog against most CONMEBOL teams. Evidence update: The FIFA source confirms this is a CONMEBOL World Cup qualifier format with a single league, implying a strong opponent pool and low baseline win probability. The squad/injury-related sources suggest Bolivia has had fitness concerns, which modestly lowers win chances. The altitude-related AP/Yahoo items indicate Bolivia may have a home-field advantage if the match is in Bolivia, which raises the probability somewhat, but not enough to overcome the overall underdog status. The fixture list source confirms Bolivia has a scheduled qualifier in mid-October 2025, but not a favorable matchup by itself. Synthesis: Combining a low base rate with a modest home/altitude uplift and some injury uncertainty yields a win probability around 22%. Calibration check: Out of 100 similar forecasts, Bolivia would win roughly 22 times, which seems plausible for a single CONMEBOL qualifier involving Bolivia.

question:
Will Tesla (TSLA) say "Diner" during earnings call?

Description: The Tesla (TSLA) 2025 Q3 Earnings Announcement is scheduled to take place on October 22, 2025, at 5:30 PM ET. (https://ir.tesla.com/press-release/tesla-third-quarter-2025-production-deliveries-deployments)

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during this event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A Q&A event will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitely cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by the date the Q4 earnings call begins, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, someone on the Q3 2025 Tesla earnings call must utter the exact term "Diner" (including plural/possessive or as part of a compound like "Tesla Diner"). Tesla has recently launched a high-profile Tesla Diner in Hollywood, making the term highly salient and likely to be referenced in prepared remarks or Q&A.

Base rate: On major Tesla earnings calls, ordinary nouns tied to current products, projects, or recent news often get mentioned, especially if they are in the company’s investor materials. A generic term like "diner" would usually be uncommon absent a specific Tesla-related project, but here there is one.

Evidence update: The strongest evidence is Tesla’s own materials and widespread coverage confirming the Tesla Diner opened in July 2025 and appears in Tesla’s Q2 2025 update deck. That makes it more likely the investor relations team, Elon Musk, or analysts will reference it during the Q3 call. The Q2 update explicitly includes "TESLA DINER" in the presentation, which increases the chance it is part of Tesla’s narrative. The earnings call transcript from prior quarters shows Tesla calls do include discussion of recent products and initiatives, so a newly opened, branded location is plausible call content. On the other hand, earnings calls can stay focused on deliveries, margins, FSD, robotaxi, and energy, so there is still some chance the diner is omitted.

Synthesis: The presence of an actual Tesla-branded diner, plus its appearance in Tesla materials and broad media attention, pushes probability well above a neutral base rate. I would expect mention in either prepared remarks or a Q&A answer, though not with certainty.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions about Tesla earnings calls with a recently launched, company-branded physical location already featured in investor materials, I’d expect "Diner" to be spoken roughly 87 times.

question:

label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Player H to win, H must be in the 2025 Tokyo main draw, survive a 28-player WTA 500 field, and win five straight matches. The market also resolves NO immediately if H is not in the draw or becomes unable to win under tournament rules. Base rate: For any specific named player in a WTA 500, the chance of winning is usually low because many strong players compete; for a non-elite or unconfirmed entrant it is far below 1%. Evidence update: The provided draw documents show the field and seeds, but none identify who "Player H" is. The main draw PDF lists players such as Jasmine Paolini, Ekaterina Alexandrova, Karolina Muchova, Diana Shnaider, etc., but no mapping to Player H is given. The qualifying draw likewise does not resolve Player H. Since the market is about a specific listed player and that identity is absent from the evidence, the most likely case is that H is either not in the draw or is not among the listed contenders, which strongly pushes toward NO. There is some residual chance that Player H corresponds to an unrecognized entrant or a player not obvious from the excerpts, but without a clear draw position or evidence of superior strength, the win probability remains tiny. Synthesis: Start from a low base rate for any single player in a WTA 500, then reduce further because the available evidence does not support H being a likely champion and may not even confirm H's participation. Calibration check: Out of 100 similar predictions at 0.07%, I would expect well under one YES, which fits this case.

question:
Will Elon Musk post 500+ tweets from November 7 to November 14, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from November 7 12:00 PM ET to November 14, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, Elon Musk would need to average at least about 71 counted posts per day over the 7-day window (500+ total from Nov 7 12:00 PM ET to Nov 14 12:00 PM ET). That is extremely high even for an unusually active account, especially under the market’s counting rules that exclude replies unless they appear as main-feed posts. Base rate: In recent observed periods, Musk often posts heavily, but 500+ counted posts in one week is still a rare extreme tail outcome. A rough prior for surpassing 500 in a 7-day span is low, likely in the low single digits percent or below. Evidence update: The France 24 piece and other sources indicate Musk can average more than 100 interactions per day, which supports the possibility of very high activity, but that average includes replies and interactions, not necessarily counted main-feed posts, and it reflects a specific period that may not generalize. The YouTube/market references show that high brackets like 580+ exist, confirming the market contemplates such extremes, but that is not evidence they occur often. The historical pattern suggests Musk’s posting is volatile and can spike, yet 500+ counted posts requires sustained near-constant posting for a full week, which is uncommon. Synthesis: Starting from a low base rate and adjusting slightly upward for his demonstrated capacity for intense posting, I still land well below 5%. Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 1.27%, I would expect about 1 to 2 YES outcomes, which feels consistent with how often such an extreme posting week should occur.

rationale: Decompose: A YES requires that in the next qualifying PMQs session Starmer says the exact phrase “Mr. Speaker” at least 30 times, as counted in the live/broadcast event. Base rate: In a standard half-hour PMQs, a prime minister or opposition leader typically makes many direct references to the Speaker; 30+ is high but plausible, especially for a combative session with multiple answers and interruptions. Evidence update: The Parliament and PMQs references confirm the format, duration, and that the Speaker is addressed throughout the session ([6], [7], [10], [12], [18], [19]). The YouTube PMQs transcript snippet shows Starmer already using “Mr. Speaker” in a live PMQs setting ([1]), supporting that this is normal speech in this venue. Hansard contribution counts suggest Starmer’s PMQs interventions can be numerous and lengthy across sessions ([16]). I do not have direct evidence that he reliably reaches 30 mentions every session, but the setting makes it substantially more likely than not. Synthesis: Starting from a moderate base rate for a long, repetitive parliamentary exchange, the live PMQs format and Starmer’s frequent addressed repetitions push the probability upward. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions, I would expect about 71 to resolve YES, which feels plausible though somewhat optimistic given session-to-session variability.

question:

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from November 18 12:00 PM ET to November 25, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For a YES outcome, Elon Musk must post between 240 and 259 counted X posts in the 7-day window from Nov 18, 2025 12:00 PM ET to Nov 25, 2025 12:00 PM ET, using the tracker’s rules (main feed posts, quote posts, reposts; replies only if surfaced on main feed). That implies an average of roughly 34-37 counted posts per day. Base rate: Musk has historically been an extremely high-volume poster, with some periods around 40 posts/day or more, but his activity is volatile and can swing materially. For a narrow 20-post band over a week, the probability is meaningfully lower than the probability of simply being in a broad high-activity range. Evidence update: The most relevant evidence suggests Musk has recently remained very active, including reports of about 40 posts/day in April 2025 and around 100+ interactions/day in earlier studies, which supports the possibility of a week in the 240-259 range. However, the market window is in mid/late November 2025, and the presence of public events around that time (e.g., shareholder meeting / conference appearances) could either increase posting or substitute for posting with in-person activity; the evidence does not establish a stable 34-37/day pattern. The exact band is also relatively narrow, so even if his expected count is near the target, there is substantial dispersion. Synthesis: I’d place the center of mass somewhat above 240, but not enough to make this band more likely than not; the chance of landing in 240-259 is around 28%. Calibration check: If I made 100 such predictions at 28.4%, about 28 should resolve YES; that seems plausible for this specific narrow-range question.

question:
Hull City AFC vs. Ipswich Town FC: Both Teams to Score

Description: In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Hull City AFC and Ipswich Town FC, scheduled for November 25 at 2:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Hull City AFC and Ipswich Town FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.efl.com/
label: YES
rationale: Decompose: For both teams to score, Hull need at least 1 goal and Ipswich need at least 1 goal in 90 minutes plus stoppage time. The main drivers are each side’s scoring rate, each side’s chance of conceding, and any lineup/injury effects that suppress attacking output. Base rate: In Championship matches, BTTS is often near the mid-50s to low-60s, especially when both teams are reasonably capable in attack. A neutral prior around 0.57 is appropriate. Evidence update: The strongest quantitative signal is recent/seasonal scoring and conceding profiles. Hull’s season scoring rate is modest at 0.96 goals per game, but they also concede 1.17 per game, which supports opponent scoring. Ipswich’s away profile is stronger on attack and their conceded rate is similar to Hull’s home concession rate, also supporting BTTS. The FootyStats-style matchup data shows both teams concede 0.9 per match and each has a 40% clean-sheet rate in the cited split, which pushes toward a moderate BTTS probability rather than a high one. The 365Scores snippet indicates recent BTTS rates around 40% for Hull and 60% for Ipswich, with xG values around 1.2 and 1.8, suggesting both have realistic scoring paths. Head-to-head snippets include several 1-0 and 0-0 type results, which slightly temper optimism, though the sample is old and small. Injury news removes some attacking/creative pieces on both sides, which is a mild downward adjustment. Synthesis: Starting from ~0.57, the balance of evidence nudges slightly upward because both teams’ concession rates and Ipswich’s stronger attacking indicators offset Hull’s relatively low scoring average. The injury situation and some low-scoring H2H history prevent a larger increase. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 60.8%, I’d expect about 61 BTTS outcomes, which feels consistent with the evidence mix and uncertainty.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

rationale: Decompose: A YES requires that someone on the earnings call says either "Chipotle Lane" or "Chipotlane" (including plural/possessive variants). Chipotle has a strong, recurring business emphasis on this format, and the term is likely to come up when discussing new unit openings, throughput, digital pickup, or capital expenditures. Base rate: On a quarterly call for a company whose strategy prominently features a named store format, the chance the format name is mentioned is high, especially when it appears in prior earnings materials and investor communications. Evidence update: The company’s own 2025 results release highlights that over 80% of new openings have a Chipotlane, making it a material KPI and a natural talking point [8]. The 2024 and 2023 filings repeatedly describe Chipotlanes as a strategic growth lever [7], and recent investor materials and third-party coverage also frame it as central to the company’s expansion narrative [4], [13]. Prior earnings call transcript listings indicate this is a standard earnings-call setting where management discusses operational and growth metrics [5], [11]. Because the term is already embedded in Chipotle’s investor language, the probability of mention is substantially above a generic earnings-call baseline. I do not go to near-certainty because management could discuss the format using a different phrasing or focus more on sales/margins than store-format terminology. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 91%, about 91 should resolve YES; that seems plausible here given how central Chipotlane is to Chipotle’s growth story.

question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be 65°F or higher on November 26?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 26 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, LaGuardia’s recorded high on Nov 26, 2025 must reach at least 65°F. This depends on late-November NYC weather being unusually mild for one day, with sufficient warmth arriving despite climatological cooling.

Base rate: In New York City, late November highs are typically in the 50s, and 65°F+ is possible but not the norm. Around Thanksgiving, days at or above 65°F happen often enough to be plausible, but not common enough to be a majority outcome.

Evidence update: The strongest evidence points to a cool-season average. WeatherSpark shows LaGuardia’s November average high around 55°F, and climate-data sources suggest November daily maxima usually trend downward through the month. The NOAA/CPC outlook-related items indicate near-normal to near-above-normal warmth at times, which modestly supports a warm spike, but these are broad and not specific to Nov 26. Historical Thanksgiving examples show some years reaching 65°F, but the sample is mixed and includes many sub-65°F outcomes. Overall, the evidence slightly favors NO because the date is late November and the climatological center of mass is well below 65°F.

Synthesis: Starting from a middling base rate for a 65°F+ late-November NYC high, the climatology and average high data pull the probability down, while seasonal outlooks and occasional warm Thanksgiving examples pull it up somewhat. Net effect is a sub-50% chance.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 42%, I would expect about 42 YES outcomes; that feels consistent with the uncertainty here.

Evidence update: The London climate records show December record highs around 62°F at nearby London stations, so 59°F is within the realm of historical possibility, not an extreme outlier. The November 2025 outlook in the evidence suggests mild conditions continuing into late autumn, which modestly increases the chance of an above-normal start to December, but this is weak evidence for a specific day and airport station. I also avoid over-weighting broader warming/climate-change evidence because it mostly shifts averages slightly and does not strongly determine a single-day maximum. Synthesis: Combining a low-to-moderate base rate with a small favorable weather-climate tilt yields a sub-1-in-4 chance. question:

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, PLTR must close above $150 on the final trading day of Thanksgiving week. That requires either (a) the stock already being near/above $150 entering the week, or (b) a sizable positive move during a short holiday week. Base rate: For a single large-cap stock to end a specific week above a high threshold, the prior is modest unless the stock is already well above that level. Evidence update: PLTR has been extremely strong in 2025 and multiple sources describe record highs, strong AI demand, and bullish analyst commentary, which pushes toward YES. However, the Yahoo Finance snapshot shows closes around $178-$186 in mid-October 2025, indicating the stock is indeed far above $150 well before Thanksgiving week, making the threshold easier to clear. The stock-split note suggests a 4-for-1 split on Nov. 14, 2025, which materially changes the nominal share price; if split-adjusted prices are used, the post-split price level would be around a quarter of pre-split levels, making $150 much harder to exceed unless the pre-split equivalent is around $600. Because the market explicitly resolves on split-adjusted Yahoo Finance closes, the split is the dominant factor and strongly pushes toward NO. The bullish analyst/earnings/AI evidence is real but likely insufficient to offset a 4-for-1 split effect on the nominal price threshold. Synthesis: Starting from a moderate base rate, the pre-split bullishness would imply a high probability of being above $150, but the split-adjusted resolution mechanism sharply lowers that probability. Calibration check: A probability around 12% means about 12 of 100 similar forecasts would resolve YES, which seems plausible given the split-adjustment uncertainty and the possibility that the post-split adjusted price could still be near or above $150 only in an extreme rally.

Evidence update: The source documents confirm the market uses LaGuardia/Wunderground finalized observations, but they do not provide a specific forecast for Dec 1, 2025. Historical references show LaGuardia can be much warmer on rare occasions, but that mainly indicates variability rather than a strong tilt above 47°F on this specific date. The current Wunderground snippets showing 73–84°F are clearly unrelated live-page artifacts and not informative for December. Synthesis: Given climatology, a high of 47°F or above is somewhat more likely than not, but not strongly so; December 1 in NYC often lands close to this cutoff, and modest cold-air intrusions can keep highs below it. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions for NYC Dec 1 highs crossing 47°F, I would expect about 29 to resolve YES.

question:

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from October 24 12:00 PM ET to October 31, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: A YES requires Elon Musk to accumulate 360-379 counted X posts in the 7-day window from Oct 24, 2025 12:00 PM ET to Oct 31, 2025 12:00 PM ET, counting main-feed posts, quote posts, and reposts, but not ordinary replies. That is roughly 51-54 counted posts per day, a very high sustained pace. Base rate: In prior Musk weekly-volume markets, his output has often been well below this band, though he can spike substantially. Evidence update: The available market docs mainly establish the counting rules and show that Musk’s weekly totals have ranged widely, including some high-output weeks around the low-to-mid 200s and occasional higher bursts. That supports the possibility of a surge but does not strongly indicate a sustained 360-379 week. The general pattern of Musk posting heavily on some days but not necessarily maintaining 50+ counted posts every day pulls against the target band. Synthesis: I start from a modest prior for this exact interval and adjust upward somewhat because Musk is capable of very high posting volume and the market band is not extreme relative to his upper ranges, but I keep the probability below 50% because 360-379 requires a very persistent, unusually intense posting week. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar forecasts at 27%, I would expect about 27 YES outcomes; that feels plausible for a high-but-not-common Musk volume bracket.

question:
Will George Russell finish third in the 2025 F1 Drivers Championship?

Description: This market will resolve according to the driver that finishes 3rd in the driver standings for the 2025 F1 season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As soon as the results for the final scheduled race of the 2025 F1 season are known, this market will resolve.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to place 3rd in the driver standings for the 2025 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), this market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season has not been completed by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.
label: NO
rationale: George Russell has a plausible but not dominant path to 3rd in the 2025 Drivers' Championship. Decompose: YES requires Russell to outperform at least several top contenders over the full season, stay healthy, and avoid being mathematically eliminated before season end. Base rate: a Mercedes driver finishing exactly 3rd is common enough to be credible, but Russell specifically finishing 3rd is far from the modal outcome given the presence of multiple elite drivers and at least three strong title-contender-level cars. Evidence update: Mercedes confirmed Russell as a 2025 driver with Kimi Antonelli as teammate, which supports a stable seat and opportunity to score points. Formula 1 coverage shows the 2025 season uses the standard points system, so no rule change materially alters the ranking logic. However, the cited F1 articles about championship permutations highlight that the front of the field is being fought over by Norris, Piastri, and Verstappen, implying those three are more likely than Russell to occupy the top championship slots. Russell’s recent seasons show he is competitive and has won races, but Mercedes’ 2024 form was not championship-dominant, and there is no strong evidence here that Mercedes has definitively become the second-best or best package in 2025. That leaves Russell as a meaningful but not favorite candidate for 3rd. Synthesis: I weight the chance that Russell finishes exactly 3rd at a bit below even odds, around 41%. Calibration check: if I made 100 similar predictions at 41%, about 41 should resolve YES.

question:
Will between 75-79 senators vote "Yea" on an Epstein disclosure bill?

Description: This market will resolve to the number of senators who vote “Yea” on the first U.S. Senate floor vote held on any bill, measure, or resolution which mandates, compels, or formally calls for the public release of documents related to Jeffrey Epstein.

The vote count refers to the first final vote on passage in the full chamber, not committee votes or procedural motions

If no such vote is held by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No vote by December 31”.

The resolution sources will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For 75-79 senators to vote yea, the bill must reach the floor, survive any procedural hurdles, and attract a very broad bipartisan coalition. In a 100-seat Senate, that requires roughly three-quarters support, which is unusually high for controversial or politically loaded legislation.

Base rate: Final Senate passage votes with 75+ yeas are rare. Most successful Senate bills pass with simple-majority margins, and even broadly supported measures often clear with far fewer than 75 votes.

Evidence update: The strongest update is that there already was a Senate vote on an Epstein-related amendment and it failed 51-49 (doc 001), showing the chamber is polarized and far from the 75-79 range on at least one closely related vehicle. However, there is also evidence that a dedicated Epstein Files Transparency Act exists in the Senate (doc 002) and that the House advanced a related bill with overwhelming support (doc 004, doc 013), which increases the chance the Senate could eventually vote on a disclosure measure and that the issue has bipartisan salience. Still, bipartisan salience does not imply 75-79 yea votes; the Senate’s partisan split and the 51-49 table motion suggest a much lower ceiling for support on this issue. I also note that the market counts the first final passage vote on any qualifying Senate measure, which reduces ambiguity but does not materially raise the probability of reaching 75-79.

Synthesis: Starting from a low base rate for 75+ yea Senate outcomes, I move up somewhat because the Epstein issue has public pressure and a formal Senate bill exists, but I keep the probability well below 50% because the only observed Senate floor action is narrowly divided and indicates limited support. My estimate is that if I made 100 such forecasts, about 18 would resolve YES.

Calibration check: 18% feels plausible given the rarity of supermajority Senate passage votes and the current evidence of polarization.

question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be between 46-47°F on November 18?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 18 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, LaGuardia’s reported daily high on Nov 18, 2025 must finalize as 46 or 47°F. That requires a fairly specific outcome within a narrow 2°F bin, and the question is about the day’s maximum, not the afternoon average.

Base rate: For mid-November in New York City, daily highs are typically in the 50s, with a meaningful but not dominant chance of cooler 40s. A 46–47°F high is plausible but not common enough to be the modal outcome.

Evidence update: The climatological context points mildly toward cooler-than-normal conditions. NOAA/forecast outlook sources suggest the Northeast may be cooler than average in November, and a FOX 5 report specifically mentions a cold spell giving NYC its first taste of winter around Nov. 7, implying the period can feature below-normal temperatures. However, these are broad seasonal or early-month signals, not a precise forecast for Nov. 18. The strongest climatological clue is that LaGuardia’s November average high is around the mid-50s, so 46–47°F is below normal but not extreme. Because the market resolves to whole degrees, the exact 46–47 bin is only a small slice of plausible outcomes even if the day is cool.

Synthesis: Starting from a modest base rate for a specific 2°F interval, then nudging upward slightly for seasonal coolness, I still land well below 50%. The most likely outcomes remain other bins in the 40s or 50s rather than exactly 46–47°F.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar forecasts at 8.7%, I would expect about 9 to resolve YES, which feels about right for this narrow temperature bin on a specific date.

question:
Will LLA win the most seats in the Chamber of Deputies following the 2025 Argentina election?

Description: The 2025 election for half of the seats in Argentina's Chamber of Deputies is scheduled to be held on October 26, 2025.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of the seats that are contested in this election.

If voting in the next Argentine election for the Chamber of Deputies does not occur by January 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the case of a tie between this party and any other for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation comes first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition. 

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Argentina's government (e.g. via https://www.electoral.gob.ar/nuevo/index.php).
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: For LLA to win the most seats among the 127 Chamber of Deputies seats contested in 2025, it needs to either outperform all other lists nationally or at least tie for first and benefit from the alphabetical tiebreak. The key drivers are its current governing status, seat exposure, alliance strategy with PRO, and whether a fragmented opposition can split the anti-LLA vote.

Base rate: In Argentine midterms, the governing coalition often performs competitively but does not always top the national seat count. However, a first-term president with strong brand visibility and favorable seat exposure has a meaningful chance. A rough prior for the incumbent president’s main electoral vehicle finishing first in seats is around the mid-40s to low-50s percent before current-cycle specifics.

Evidence update: The evidence points moderately toward YES. Multiple sources indicate LLA is organized nationally, is the only force present in all districts, and has formed an alliance with PRO, which should improve its seat efficiency and vote aggregation. The Europarl briefing explicitly says LLA has a good chance of increasing its congressional seats and has a favorable renewal profile, with relatively few seats at risk compared with the opposition. That does not guarantee first place, but it materially improves the odds. On the other hand, the opposition, especially peronism, remains a large and entrenched force, and provincial alliances can complicate the national seat race. Because the market is about seats won in the Chamber, not vote share, district-by-district fragmentation matters, and a well-coordinated opposition could still edge LLA.

Synthesis: Starting from a moderate base rate, the favorable seat exposure, national presence, and alliance with PRO shift the probability upward, but not overwhelmingly because opposition strength and Argentina’s fragmented electoral geography remain substantial. I would price LLA as somewhat more likely than not to win the most seats.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar forecasts at 58%, I would expect LLA to win the most seats in about 58 cases, which feels plausible given the current evidence and uncertainty.

question:
Will Keir Starmer say "Wales" during the next Prime Minister's Questions?

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. label: YES

Base rate: In a normal PMQs, the Prime Minister often mentions at least one UK nation or region, especially when responding to opposition questions or discussing public services, transport, steel, devolution, or budgets. For a common geographic/political term like “Wales,” the prior should be fairly high.

Evidence update: The provided materials point strongly toward an upcoming PMQs where Starmer is expected to appear ([6], [7], [8], [18]). Several recent government and parliamentary items prominently feature Wales-related policy and debate ([1], [2], [3], [5], [11], [12], [14], [20]), which increases the chance that Wales will be topical in the chamber or at least in the broader political environment. However, these are only indirect signals; they do not guarantee the word will be spoken in the specific PMQs event. The term is also not so rare that it requires a special prompt, and Starmer often uses nation names naturally in answers.

Synthesis: Starting from a high base rate for a common term in PMQs, the Wales-specific context nudges probability upward, but not to near certainty because question content is unpredictable and the market is about one specific event. I therefore land in the mid-to-high 80s.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar forecasts at 0.84, roughly 84 would resolve YES. That feels plausible for a common nation name in a live PMQs appearance.

Evidence update: the official Ligue 1 schedule confirms the fixture and timing, which removes schedule uncertainty but does not improve Toulouse’s chance. Recent preview/injury items suggest Toulouse have had some squad absences and are facing a high-quality opponent; that nudges slightly toward NO. No strong evidence in the provided sources indicates Toulouse being favored. Synthesis: starting from a sub-35% away-underdog base rate and applying a modest negative adjustment yields about 31%. Calibration check: if I made 100 similar predictions at 31.2%, roughly 31 should resolve YES.

question:
Will Person B come in third in the 1st round of the 2025 Chilean presidential election?

Description: The 2025 Chilean general election is scheduled for 16 November 2025. The President of Chile is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the third-greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Chilean Electoral Service (https://www.servel.cl/).
label: YES
rationale: Decompose: For Person B to finish third, they need to be behind exactly two candidates in the first-round vote count. In a fragmented Chilean presidential field with eight candidates, this is plausible if the top three are concentrated among the main contenders and the remaining vote is split. Base rate: In multi-candidate presidential races with a clear top tier, the candidate currently polling around third place often finishes third, but there is meaningful volatility, especially with compulsory voting and undecided/late-deciding voters. Evidence update: Current polling and analyst summaries consistently place Jeannette Jara and José Antonio Kast at the top, with Evelyn Matthei, Johannes Kaiser, and Franco Parisi competing for the next positions. The polling snapshots show a relatively stable top three/tier, and several sources indicate Matthei is often third in averages while Kaiser/Parisi can challenge her, which makes a third-place finish for the relevant candidate reasonably likely. However, the exact identity of Person B is not provided here, so I cannot condition on a specific candidate’s polling position; that uncertainty lowers confidence. Chile’s first-round system and historical results suggest no one wins outright, making third place meaningful and likely to be determined by the same polling hierarchy. Calibration check: A 0.67 probability means I’d expect this to happen about two-thirds of the time in similar cases; given the current polling structure and candidate field, that feels slightly conservative but defensible.

question:
Will Jeannette Jara win less than 20% of votes in the first round?

Description: First-round presidential elections are scheduled to be held in Chile on November 16, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the vote share Jeannette Jara wins in the first round of the 2025 Chilean presidential election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the proportion of votes won by Jeannette Jara, not any coalition of which she may be a part.

If the results of the election aren’t known by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.  

This market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Chilean Government, specifically the Chilean Electoral Service (Servicio Electoral de Chile, Servel) (https://www.servel.cl/).
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Jeannette Jara to win under 20% in the first round, she would need to collapse from being a top-tier contender to a very weak showing, despite being the left coalition’s nominee in a polarized field. Base rate: In Chilean presidential first rounds, leading candidates typically land well above 20%, and candidates from major blocs usually clear that threshold unless the race is unusually fragmented or their campaign implodes. Evidence update: The October 2025 polling cited by Statista shows Jara around the mid-20s in multiple surveys (e.g., 24%, 25.4%, 26%), with one lower outlier at 18.7% and another around 12% in an incomplete/unclear entry; overall, the polling cluster supports a vote share above 20%. Wikipedia evidence indicates she won the left primary and was officially nominated, which increases her visibility and coalition backing. Historical turnout and prior election data suggest Chilean first-round vote shares for major candidates are usually well above 20%, making a sub-20% result unlikely absent a major late campaign reversal. Synthesis: Starting from a low single-digit base probability for a top-party candidate to fall below 20%, the polling evidence pushes somewhat toward YES because some polls are near the threshold, but the balance of evidence still favors NO. Calibration check: If I made 100 such predictions at 4.9%, I would expect about 5 to resolve YES, which feels plausible here.

question:
Rangers FC vs. AS Roma: Both Teams to Score

Description: In the upcoming UEFA Europa League game between Rangers FC and AS Roma, scheduled for November 6 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Rangers FC and AS Roma each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.uefa.com/
label: YES
rationale: Decompose: For BTTS to land, both teams need at least one goal in 90 minutes. That requires at least moderate attacking quality from both sides and no shutout by either defense. Base rate: In Europa League matches between reasonably competitive sides, BTTS is often near the mid-50s, but not universally so; a neutral prior around 0.56 seems reasonable. Evidence update: Several previews point to Roma being favorites and expect a low-scoring match ([1], [5], [11], [13]), which pushes somewhat toward NO because a 0-1 or 0-2 type game is plausible. However, there is also explicit support for Roma scoring via anytime scorer picks and over-2.5/1-2 style predictions ([11], [12]), and Roma’s attack-minded setup is mentioned ([13]); that nudges toward YES. Rangers at home in Europe usually have enough attacking threat to get a goal, and the match context suggests both sides are capable of scoring rather than one being completely shut out. The low-scoring signals are meaningful, but they mostly affect total goals more than they eliminate BTTS. Synthesis: Starting from ~0.56, I move down for the low-scoring expectation but back up because a 1-1 or 1-2 game remains quite live. Net probability lands a bit above coin-flip, around 0.61. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 0.61, about 61 should resolve YES; that feels plausible given the mixed but slightly BTTS-favorable evidence.

question:
Will Volkan Oezdemir win by KO or TKO?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Volkan Oezdemir defeats Alonzo Menifield at UFC Fight Night: Tsarukyan vs. Hooker, scheduled for November 22, 2025, by KO or TKO, including referee stoppage, doctor stoppage, or corner stoppage. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the fight ends in a draw or by disqualification (either fighter), it will resolve "No."

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond December 6, 2025, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

Resolution source: https://www.ufc.com/events
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: A YES requires Volkan Oezdemir to win and the win to come by KO/TKO rather than decision, submission, DQ, or no contest. For this matchup, the key drivers are both fighters’ finishing profiles, durability, and the market/preview consensus on fight length and method.

Base rate: In UFC light heavyweight bouts involving established strikers with power, a KO/TKO outcome is common but far from certain. A reasonable prior for Oezdemir specifically winning by KO/TKO in a ranked matchup is slightly above even odds, because he is a known early-round finisher, but not overwhelmingly so.

Evidence update: Multiple previews describe Oezdemir as one of the division’s most dangerous early-round finishers and emphasize his cleaner striking and ability to capitalize on openings (docs 7, 8, 17). The betting market also prices him as a meaningful favorite, and the under 2.5 rounds is favored, both of which support a stoppage-heavy fight environment (docs 14, 19). At the same time, Menifield is also a power threat and has improved defensively, which adds some countervailing risk that if the fight ends early it could be by Menifield or by decision rather than a Volkan stoppage (docs 6, 16, 19). Overall, the evidence nudges toward Oezdemir by KO/TKO, but not with high confidence because both men can finish and Menifield’s recent form suggests he may be harder to put away than a pure fade would imply.

Synthesis: Starting from a moderate base rate, the market and analyst consensus on Oezdemir’s striking edge and the likely under support a KO/TKO outcome. I would still discount somewhat for Menifield’s power and improved discipline, which lowers certainty versus a pure Oezdemir win. Net probability is modestly above 0.5.

Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 0.57, I would expect about 57 to resolve YES, with a plausible range around the low-to-mid 50s to low 60s given matchup volatility.

question:

label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, App D must be the top paid iPhone app in the US App Store at exactly 12:00 PM ET on Nov 21. That requires either sustained high paid-download demand or a short-lived spike strong enough to beat all other paid apps at that moment. Base rate: the #1 paid app position is volatile and often dominated by a small set of consumer apps, games, or seasonal promotions; for a specific named app with no direct evidence of current chart leadership, the prior is modest. Evidence update: The provided materials mostly concern App Store pricing, developer rules, and general market context, not App D’s actual sales momentum. The only mildly relevant inference is that App Store pricing flexibility and policy changes can affect paid-app rankings, but nothing indicates App D is benefiting from a specific promotion, viral lift, or category tailwind. There is also no evidence that App D is currently near the top of the paid chart. Synthesis: With no direct ranking or demand evidence, I lean NO, but not strongly because paid rankings can change quickly and unknown apps can briefly reach #1 due to promotions or niche demand. question:
Will VVD win the fewest seats in the next Netherlands General Election?

Description: This market will resolve to the political party that wins the fewest number of seats among the listed parties in the Netherlands House of Representatives (Tweede Kamer der Staten-Generaal) as a result of this election.

Only listed parties will be considered. 

If voting in the Netherlands election for the House does not occur by July 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

In the case of a tie between two or more of the listed parties for the fewest seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation comes first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the listed party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, it will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Electoral Council (Kiesraad) (https://www.kiesraad.nl/).

Note: If the GL/PvdA alliance splits and announces they will not run a joint list, this market will refer to the Labor Party (PvdA).
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: For VVD to win the fewest seats among the listed parties, it must underperform the small parties that usually cluster near the bottom, such as CU, Volt, PvdD, SGP, FvD, BBB, SP, and possibly NSC if it remains listed. That requires either a sharp further collapse for VVD or a relatively strong performance by the usual smallest parties.

Base rate: In Dutch multiparty elections, established major parties like VVD rarely finish last among a list of mainstream parties; they usually remain mid-to-upper tier even when declining. The historical reference class therefore starts against VVD being the smallest.

Evidence update: The strongest evidence is current polling/projection data. Multiple 2025 projections place VVD around 14 to 20 seats, while several other listed parties are projected lower: BBB around 4 to 5, SP around 4, PvdD around 3 to 4, CU around 2 to 3, Volt around 2, and in some projections NSC at 0. This strongly suggests VVD will not be the fewest-seat party. The 2023 result also had VVD at 24 seats, far above the bottom tier, reinforcing that it is structurally larger than the likely last-place parties. There is some uncertainty because polls can move and the exact party list matters, but the gap is large enough that a last-place VVD outcome looks unlikely.

Synthesis: Combining the base rate with the polling evidence yields a low probability that VVD ends up with the fewest seats. The main reason to keep the probability above very low levels is the possibility of an extreme late collapse for VVD or an unusual surge by every smaller listed party, but that would be a substantial upset.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 72%, I would expect about 72 YES outcomes. That feels too high for this question; the evidence points more toward NO. So I lower the YES probability to a level that better reflects the strong polling disadvantage for a last-place VVD result.

question:
Will Barnsley FC vs. York City FC end in a draw?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 1, 2025
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".

Resolution source: https://www.thefa.com/competitions/thefacup
label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: A YES requires Barnsley and York to be level after 90 minutes (or the match to be canceled with no replay, which is unlikely here). Because this is an FA Cup first-round tie, a draw at full time would normally trigger a replay rather than end the competition, so the relevant event is still a 90-minute draw.

Base rate: In football, draws are common but not the most likely single outcome. For a cup match between teams from different divisions, the draw rate is usually somewhat below an even 50% chance and often sits in the low-to-mid 20s to low 30s depending on team strength and venue. A reasonable prior here is around the high 20s.

Evidence update: Barnsley are the higher-level side and are playing at home, both of which reduce draw probability relative to a neutral matchup. York’s preview frames the tie as an upset attempt, reinforcing Barnsley’s edge. Barnsley’s recent league table context suggests they are a stronger, established side, while York come from a lower tier. That pushes toward a home win rather than a draw. On the other hand, cup ties can be cautious and lower-scoring, which keeps draw probability non-trivial. The FA Cup rules evidence mainly confirms that a draw is possible and would lead to a replay, but it does not increase the chance of a draw itself.

Synthesis: Starting from a prior near 0.28 and adjusting downward for Barnsley’s home advantage and likely quality edge, but not too far because cup ties can be tighter, I arrive at about 0.238.

Calibration check: If I made 100 forecasts at 0.238, I would expect about 24 to resolve YES, which feels plausible for this matchup.

question:
Malik Washington: Anytime Touchdown

Description: In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for November 30 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Malik Washington scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Malik Washington does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take a snap, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

Resolution source: https://www.nfl.com/scores
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, Malik Washington must be active, take at least one snap, and score any qualifying touchdown (rush, reception, or return). The biggest drivers are his role in the offense/special teams, his historical touchdown rate, and whether he is likely to be game-script dependent. Base rate: For a non-primary skill-position receiver/return specialist, anytime TD probability in a single NFL game is usually well below 25%, often in the 10%-20% range depending on usage. Evidence update: The sources indicate Washington is a Dolphins WR/return specialist with receiving and return contributions, which gives him some paths to a TD, but not a high-volume scoring role. The available fantasy/odds-style material does not place him among the top anytime TD candidates, which is a modest signal against. No evidence here suggests he is inactive, so the market should not be forced to No on that basis. Synthesis: Combining a low-to-moderate base rate with limited positive role evidence yields a probability in the high teens. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 18%, I would expect about 18 to resolve YES, which feels plausible for a secondary receiver/returner with some but not dominant scoring avenues.

question:
Will Starbucks say "Comparable Sales" during earnings call?

Description: This market will resolve based on the next earnings announcement of Starbucks currently scheduled to take place on October 29, 2025 at 5:00 PM EDT.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during this event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A Q&A event will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitely cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.
label: YES
rationale: Decompose: For a YES, someone on the Starbucks earnings call must say the exact term "Comparable Sales" or an allowed plural/possessive variant. Starbucks routinely discusses same-store/comparable store sales on earnings calls, and this phrase is central to retail earnings commentary. Base rate: For a company like Starbucks, the probability that its earnings call includes this standard operating metric is very high, especially when historical transcripts repeatedly show it. Evidence update: Recent Starbucks earnings materials explicitly use "Comparable store sales" in press releases and transcripts, and multiple prior call transcripts/results pages reference the metric. The company’s investor materials repeatedly highlight comparable store sales as a core KPI, making it highly likely the term will be spoken during the upcoming call. I do not see meaningful evidence pushing toward NO, since this is standard terminology in Starbucks earnings communication. Synthesis: Combining the high base rate with strong company-specific evidence yields a very high probability, though not absolute because there is a small chance the call format or speaker wording changes and uses a synonym instead. Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 96.3%, I would expect about 96 to resolve YES, which feels appropriate here.

question:
Will Max Verstappen win the 2025 F1 Mexican Grand Prix?

Description: This is a polymarket on the winner of the 2025 F1 Mexican Grand Prix, scheduled for October 26, 2025.

If the 2025 F1 Mexican Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after December 1, 2025, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially listed in first place in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The Final Classification is typically released 30–60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA’s published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Verstappen to win, he needs to be in contention on race pace, qualify well enough to avoid traffic, avoid incidents/penalties, and beat at least several strong front-runners over a race that historically rewards track position. Base rate: Mexico has been won by Verstappen often, but not overwhelmingly in the most recent year; among the current field, the race is usually contested by multiple top teams, so a single-driver win probability in a given year is typically well below 25% unless that driver is clearly dominant. Evidence update: The strongest positive evidence is historical success at this venue: Verstappen has won five times in Mexico and is the most successful active driver there (docs 3, 10, 15). That meaningfully raises his chance versus a generic top driver. However, several pieces push down: the 2024 race was won by Sainz with Verstappen only 6th (doc 9), showing Mexico is not Verstappen-locked; the 2025 preview says Verstappen has struggled with long-run pace and calls the race difficult from P5 (doc 12, doc 18), which is a material negative for win chances; and the championship is tightly contested with McLaren drivers and Verstappen all in the mix (docs 5, 19), implying strong competition rather than a clear Verstappen edge. Weather looks mostly dry, which reduces randomness and usually favors the fastest car/driver rather than chaos (docs 1, 5), but that is only a mild factor. Synthesis: Starting from a moderate prior for a top driver at a single race, I move up for Verstappen's Mexico track record, then back down for current-weekend performance concerns and strong competition. I land near one-in-five. Calibration check: If I made 100 such predictions at 19%, I would expect about 19 wins; that feels consistent with Verstappen being one of the favorites but far from a certainty in this specific race.

question:
Will Tyler Glasnow be named the 2025 World Series MVP?

Description: This polymarket will resolve according to the player who is named the 2025 World Series MVP.

If the 2025 World Series MVP is not announced by May 1, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official announcements from MLB. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Tyler Glasnow to be named 2025 World Series MVP, the Dodgers must reach and win the World Series, Glasnow must be on the roster and contribute meaningfully, and his performance must stand out enough to beat all other candidates. Base rate: World Series MVPs are usually position players or the most dominant starting pitcher in the series; pitchers win often enough to matter, but any specific pitcher has a low ex ante chance because the award is shared across an entire roster and depends on usage, health, and results. Evidence update: The Dodgers appear to be a strong contender to reach the World Series, which helps Glasnow’s chance versus the entire league. However, the field of likely Dodgers MVP candidates is broad, and high-profile bats or other pitchers could easily outshine him. The cited preview material specifically frames other Dodgers pitchers like Blake Snell as dark-horse MVP candidates, and the Dodgers’ rotation includes several elite arms, which dilutes Glasnow’s share. Glasnow’s own recent health/availability history also adds uncertainty, even though reporting suggests he is a key postseason piece. Synthesis: Conditional on the Dodgers winning the World Series, Glasnow still needs a fairly narrow path to MVP—likely multiple dominant starts and a series outcome that makes him the clear narrative choice. That makes the probability materially below 10%, though not negligible given his talent and the Dodgers’ strong championship odds. Calibration check: If I made 100 such forecasts at about 4.8%, roughly 5 should resolve YES. That feels about right.

question:
Will Palantir (PLTR) finish week of November 17 above $172?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history
label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, PLTR must close above $172 on the final trading day of the specified week. That requires either a sustained late-week rally or a sharp gap higher that is maintained into the close. Palantir is a highly volatile large-cap growth stock, but $172 is an extremely high weekly-close threshold relative to typical week-to-week moves, so the bar is steep.

Base rate: For a single stock to finish a specific week above a very high strike-like level, the prior probability is usually low unless the threshold is near the prevailing price. For a name like PLTR, weekly closes above such an elevated level are uncommon absent a major catalyst.

Evidence update: The cited evidence points to strong business momentum and bullish analyst commentary, including a potential major Army contract, strong Q2/Q3 2025 results, and upbeat growth narratives. That supports upside and makes a rally more plausible than for a generic stock. However, there is also explicit valuation concern from Bloomberg and others, which argues against assuming continued vertical appreciation. The analyst target cited at $120 is far below $172, suggesting the market viewed that level as well above consensus fundamental valuation. The evidence overall is bullish on the company but not enough to imply a near-certain weekly close above $172.

Synthesis: I start from a low base rate and move it upward modestly because PLTR can move violently on news and has strong sentiment tailwinds. But I keep the probability low because the threshold is very high and the provided evidence does not indicate a specific imminent catalyst sufficient to justify such a close. My estimate is around 4.1%.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 4.1%, I would expect about 4 YES outcomes, which feels plausible for a high-bar weekly stock-price question.

question:
Will App G be #1 Free App in the US Apple App Store on October 24?

question:
Europe World Cup Qualifiers Parlay

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met by November 13, 2025, 11:59 PM ET:

- France wins against Ukraine
- Portugal wins against the Republic of Ireland
- England wins against Serbia

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Draws will not be considered wins.

If a game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If a game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met.

This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time of the respective games.
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: this parlay needs three separate 90-minute wins by strong European teams: France over Ukraine, Portugal over Ireland, and England over Serbia. A YES requires all three to land, so the joint probability is the product of three fairly high but still uncertain match outcomes.

Base rate: for top-tier national teams at home in World Cup qualifying, a single straight win is often around 70%-80% depending on opponent strength. Three-leg parlays of this type usually end up well below 50% because the probabilities multiply.

Evidence update: France are described as strong at home and favored in previews against Ukraine, which pushes their win probability up. Portugal vs Ireland also looks favorable for Portugal, with Ireland having some recent competitiveness but Portugal generally the stronger side. England vs Serbia is also favorable for England; the squad note says England had already secured qualification, which can slightly reduce urgency/rotation risk, but they remain the stronger team. The schedule evidence confirms the match is indeed taking place on Nov. 13, 2025, so there is no postponement issue apparent. The standings and recent results suggest all three favorites are in reasonably good form.

Synthesis: a plausible set of win probabilities might be France 0.78, Portugal 0.73, England 0.76. Multiplying gives about 0.433. Adjusting slightly upward for the favorable preview consensus but downward for parlay uncertainty and possible rotation/variance yields a final estimate around 0.71? Wait—that would be too high for the parlay. Calibration check says if I predicted 0.71, about 71 of 100 such three-leg parlays should win, which is far too many. A more realistic calibrated probability is closer to the low-to-mid 0.40s. Final estimate: 0.43.

question:

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if both the US Senate pass a government funding bill by October 2, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US Government. However, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, the Senate must pass a government funding bill by Oct. 2, 2025. That requires (1) a bill or continuing resolution to be on the floor, (2) enough bipartisan support to clear the Senate’s 60-vote hurdle if subject to cloture, and (3) passage before the deadline. Base rate: In shutdown-deadline episodes, Congress often does pass a short-term CR at the last moment, but Senate passage is far from guaranteed when partisan conflict is high. A rough prior in a late-September funding standoff is around the mid-40s to low-50s. Evidence update: The strongest update is negative. Multiple contingency plans for FY2026 from federal agencies imply planners were preparing for a lapse beginning Oct. 1, which is consistent with a serious risk that no funding bill would be enacted in time. Time reporting says a key Senate vote failed, leaving no clear path to avert an Oct. 1 shutdown, and NTEU explicitly says the Senate rejected the House-passed funding bill on Sept. 23. That materially lowers the chance of passage by Oct. 2. On the positive side, the House did pass a clean CR extending funding to Nov. 21, and the Senate calendar had sessions on Sept. 29 and 30, so there was still procedural room for a late compromise. But the evidence suggests the Senate was in a failed-stalemate state rather than a near-deal state. Synthesis: Starting from a moderate base rate, the failed Senate vote and shutdown contingency preparations pull the probability down more than the remaining legislative time pulls it up. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions in this situation, I would expect about 41 to resolve YES.

However, the event is likely scripted and moderated, which reduces the chance of off-topic or colorful phrasing; “stabbed in the ass” seems extremely unlikely in this venue, and I treat it as negligible. The presence of a Q&A would raise the chance somewhat, but there is no strong evidence here that the event format will elicit crime talk. Synthesis: Combining a moderate base rate for saying “crime” with a low-probability formal-event context yields a probability meaningfully below 0.5 but above the teens for either term being uttered. If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about Cuomo's appearance(s) at the listed speech. Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the stated speech will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the speech contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market. 

A “post-election speech” refers to the first live major address by Cuomo after 9 PM ET on November 4 in which he acknowledges or reacts to the election result in definitive or victory/defeat-style terms.

Speeches qualify if Cuomo explicitly claims victory or otherwise speaks as if he has won—for example, thanking supporters, referencing “our administration,” or outlining plans for office.

Remarks that focus on continued vote counting, urge patience, or avoid characterizing the outcome do not qualify, nor do interviews, press gaggles, recorded statements, or social-media posts.

To qualify, the speech must last at least five consecutive minutes of continuous live delivery.

If multiple appearances occur, the first qualifying live address will be used. If none occurs by November 5, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No.”

label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For a YES, Cuomo must first give a qualifying first post-election live speech after 9 PM ET on Nov 4, 2025, lasting at least five minutes, and within that speech utter the phrase "next mayor" (plural/possessive variants count, but not unrelated uses). The key uncertainty is whether he gives such a speech at all, and if so whether he uses that exact phrase. Base rate: In close mayoral races, losing candidates often give concession speeches that may reference the winner as "the next mayor," but the exact wording is far from guaranteed; many speeches use names, congratulations, or broader language instead. Evidence update: Cuomo has been active in campaign messaging and media appearances, suggesting he is likely to have a public reaction if the result is decisive. However, the provided evidence does not indicate a planned victory speech, and polling evidence suggests he is trailing, making a concession speech more likely than a victory speech. In a concession, saying "next mayor" is plausible but still not standard enough to be likely. The phrase is common in political speech, but not a default formula. Synthesis: I start from a modest base rate for an exact phrase in a qualifying speech and slightly increase it because a concession context makes the phrase somewhat natural if he acknowledges the winner. I do not increase much because there is substantial risk he gives no qualifying speech, gives a brief statement, or uses different wording. Calibration check: A 22% forecast means about 22 of 100 similar cases would resolve YES; that feels plausible given the combination of a likely concession scenario and the specificity of the wording requirement.

question:

Synthesis: Given his speaking style, a one-week hit is plausible but still more likely than not to miss because many weeks pass without the exact term appearing in recorded remarks. Synthesis: Starting from a low base rate and adjusting upward slightly for Musk’s propensity for bursts of heavy posting, I still land below 20%.
