Pluralization/possessive of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about Trump's appearance at the specified event on November 5, 2025 (https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/topic/calendar/). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by November 5, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, Trump must mention the exact term “UFC” during his scheduled America Business Forum appearance on Nov. 5, 2025, and the event must actually occur/be aired. Base rate: In a typical Trump speech, explicit mentions of UFC are possible but not common unless the topic is sports, Dana White, or fighting. Evidence update: The event is real and Trump is scheduled to speak, which supports a valid opportunity. Multiple sources suggest the forum includes sports icons and global athletes, making a UFC mention somewhat more plausible than in a generic business speech. However, the available descriptions emphasize business, leadership, trade, and investment rather than combat sports. Trump has a strong Dana White/UFC association and has referenced UFC in prior remarks, which nudges toward YES, but there is no direct indication he will mention the acronym at this event. Synthesis: I’d assign a modestly below-even chance because the topic alignment is weak-to-moderate rather than strong. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions, I’d expect about 34 to resolve YES.

question:

Pluralization/possessive of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Zohran Mamdani. All such events featuring both on this date count. label: YES
rationale: Decompose: For YES, Trump must publicly say either “Little” or “Liddle” during the live, qualifying Mamdani event(s) on Nov. 21, 2025. The key driver is whether he uses a nickname-style insult in a live setting, which he has done frequently in the past. Base rate: Trump has a strong history of using derisive nicknames for political opponents, and the specific family of terms “Little/Liddle” is well-established in his rhetoric. Evidence update: The supplied articles show a hostile Trump-Mamdani dynamic, making an insult more plausible than for a neutral meeting. Separate evidence shows Trump has used “Liddle” as an alternate spelling in prior nicknames and has a pattern of using “Little” for rivals. However, the meeting is also a formal White House appearance, which somewhat reduces the chance of overt name-calling compared with rallies or interviews. Synthesis: The prior for Trump using some nickname/insult in this context is meaningfully above 50%, and the specific target terms are among his known vocabulary. I therefore lean YES, but not overwhelmingly, because formal optics could suppress it. Calibration check: At 62%, I’d expect about 62 such cases out of 100 to resolve YES, which feels plausible here.

question:
Will Russia capture Myrnohrad by November 7?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the intersection between Vatutina Vulytsya and Puhachova Vulytsya located in Myrnohrad by November 7, 2025, at 11:59 PM ET.

The intersection station will be considered captured if any part of the intersection is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by November 7, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the intersection, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Intersection Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad+intersection+zoom.png

Intersection Location in Myrnohrad: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad+intersection.jpeg

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.
label: YES
rationale: Decompose: For YES, Russia must have actual control of the specified Myrnohrad intersection by the deadline, either reflected on ISW/DeepStateMap or established via credible reporting/settlement. Base rate: When Russian forces are already inside a town and advancing, but the target is a specific urban intersection, capture within days to weeks is plausible but far from certain because urban pockets can hold out and map confirmation can lag. Evidence update: The late-October to early-November reporting shows repeated Russian advances into southeastern/southern/eastern Myrnohrad, with ISW noting Russian forces operating with increasing comfort in Pokrovsk and advancing in Myrnohrad. That is a meaningful upward update toward YES. However, ISW also explicitly says the advances are unlikely to cause an immediate collapse, the tactical picture remains porous/unclear, and the target is a precise intersection rather than the town generally. The remaining time window is short, but the operational momentum is strongly in Russia’s favor. Synthesis: I start from a moderate base rate and move upward because Russian presence in Myrnohrad is already established and expanding. I do not go much above the mid-50s because specific intersection capture is still uncertain and urban control can be fragmented. Calibration check: Out of 100 similar forecasts at 0.56, I would expect roughly 56 to resolve YES.

The show premiered Oct. 9, so the relevant window includes only a partial first week and likely competes against established titles with full-week momentum. Nothing in the evidence indicates exceptional breakout buzz or that it has enough mass-market reach to beat all other English-language TV shows worldwide. question:
Will Trump send the national guard to Chicago by December 31?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump or his administration takes a formal action or makes an official announcement directing the deployment of National Guard troops to the listed city by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No.”
A qualifying announcement or action will count, regardless of if/when the deployment actually occurs, or if the deployment is delayed, suspended, or subsequently blocked by judicial or other actions.

Qualifying actions include but are not limited to:
- A Presidential action (e.g., Executive Order, Presidential Memorandum) that directs or announces National Guard deployment to the listed city.
- An official White House/DoD/National Guard Bureau press release, fact sheet, or order explicitly stating that National Guard troops will be deployed to the listed city.
- A Secretary of Defense or NGB directive/order that calls up or assigns National Guard forces to the listed city, including under Title 32 (state control with federal funding) or Title 10 (federalized).
- An official federal announcement that specific state Guard units (including the listed city National Guard or out-of-state Guard) are being sent to the listed city.

Non-qualifying actions include:
- Informal announcements/social media posts which do not constitute a formal policy announcement 
- Ambiguous remarks, “we’re considering it,” broad statements like “ready to go anywhere,” or contingent plans without a clear directive to the listed city.
- Generic posture changes (e.g., standing up quick-reaction forces “nationwide”) that do not explicitly name the listed city as a deployment location.
- A state-only activation by the listed city's state Governor without any official Trump administration action or announcement about sending the Guard to the listed city.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: YES
rationale:
Base rate: Presidents do sometimes authorize National Guard deployments to major cities during unrest, immigration enforcement, or perceived federal-security threats, but a formal, city-specific announcement is still a relatively high-salience action. Starting prior is modestly below even.

Evidence update: The strongest update is that multiple 2025 reports and official city statements indicate Trump has already been publicly threatening or pushing to send troops/Guard to Chicago, and there is at least one White House document showing a formal National Guard federal-service action in a related domestic security context. The Chicago-specific items suggest this is not hypothetical chatter but an active policy idea with real administrative movement. The fact that Chicago officials are issuing protective orders in response to an anticipated deployment also supports that the threat is concrete. On the other hand, the evidence provided does not include a clear, explicit federal directive naming Chicago yet, so some uncertainty remains about whether a qualifying formal announcement will occur by year-end.

Synthesis: Given Trump’s demonstrated willingness to use Guard deployments domestically, the repeated Chicago-specific threats, and the apparent escalation in federal activity around Chicago, I put the probability meaningfully above 50%, but not near certainty because the market requires a formal action/announcement, not just rhetoric.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar forecasts at 71%, about 71 should resolve YES. That feels plausible here.

question:

If at any point it becomes impossible for this pair to win the 2025 Beach Volleyball World Championships (Men’s Tournament) per the official rules, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the FIVB. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
label: NO
rationale:
Base rate: Winning a Beach Volleyball World Championship is difficult even for elite teams; among 48 teams, a top pair might have something like a low-teens chance before considering field strength. Cherif Younousse & Ahmed Tijan are clearly one of the strongest teams in the world, so their prior is materially above average.

Evidence update: The strongest updates are positive. They are ranked number two in the world in mid-October 2025, have recent elite-level wins (Newport Beach, Rio), and FIVB describes them as among the world’s elite with a fourth-place finish at Paris 2024. They also have proven championship pedigree and are directly entered into the Adelaide 2025 event. However, the World Championships field is deep, single-elimination after pool play, and even top-2 teams can easily fail to convert a title in a single tournament. The evidence supports contender status, not favoritism strong enough to make them more likely than not.

Synthesis: Starting from a low-teens base rate for a top contender, the ranking and recent form push them upward, but not dramatically enough to overcome the inherent variance of a one-off world championship. My calibrated estimate is about 14%.

Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 14%, I would expect roughly 14 winners; that feels plausible for a team of this caliber in this field.

question:
Will Aston Martin get pole position at the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix?

Description: This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix, scheduled for Nov 29, 2025.

If the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after Dec 6, 2025, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

Resolution source: https://www.formula1.com/en/results/2025/races
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Aston Martin to get pole, one of its drivers must set the fastest official qualifying time at the 2025 Qatar GP. That requires Aston Martin to outperform the field in a single qualifying session at a track where pole is usually contested by the top teams. Base rate: Aston Martin has been a low-probability pole contender in the modern F1 era, especially compared with Red Bull, McLaren, Ferrari, and Mercedes. A reasonable prior for any specific non-favorite team taking pole at a given race is low, and for Aston Martin specifically at Qatar it is very low. Evidence update: The schedule confirms the race and qualifying are set to occur normally on Nov 29, 2025, so there is no cancellation/reschedule signal pushing to “Other.” The Qatar circuit historically has been dominated by stronger front-running teams, with recent poles going to Red Bull and Mercedes, and the 2024 pole to Verstappen. The 2025 weekend notes and weather forecasts suggest dry, stable conditions, which reduces randomness from weather and tends to favor the fastest car rather than create unusual upset conditions. There is no evidence here that Aston Martin has exceptional pace or a special qualifying advantage at Lusail. The presence of Aston Martin in practice imagery is not meaningful evidence of pole potential. Synthesis: I start from a very small base rate and make only a modest upward adjustment for the inherent uncertainty of motorsport qualifying. Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 1.5%, I would expect about 1-2 YES outcomes, which feels consistent with Aston Martin’s realistic pole chances here.

question:
Will the fight be won by KO or TKO?

label: YES
rationale: Base rate: UFC fights at this level end by KO/TKO somewhat more often than not, but middleweight prelims are not overwhelmingly finish-heavy. A reasonable starting prior is around 0.52-0.56. Evidence update: Ryan Loder’s recent UFC win was by KO/TKO, which supports finishing upside, and the preview material repeatedly frames him as a ground-and-pound/finishing threat. On the other hand, the same previews suggest Ismail Naurdiev has the striking edge, good takedown defense, and enough resilience to keep the bout standing, which can cut both ways: a stand-up fight increases KO/TKO chances, but also suggests a potentially more technical, less reckless contest. Loder’s low takedown accuracy and Naurdiev’s defensive metrics reduce the likelihood of a dominant grappling grind leading to a decision, modestly favoring a finish. Overall, the evidence is mildly pro-KO/TKO, but not strongly enough to push the probability much above the mid-50s. Calibration check: if I made 100 similar predictions at 55.8%, I would expect about 56 to resolve YES, which feels plausible.

question:
Will Google (GOOGL) finish week of November 10 above $285?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GOOGL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

label: YES
rationale: Decompose: For YES, GOOGL must close above 285 on the final trading day of the specified week. That requires either the stock already being near 285 going into that Friday or a meaningful positive move during the week. Base rate: For a large-cap mega-cap like Alphabet, a one-week move above a specific round-number threshold is plausible but not the default; weekly closes above a higher strike-like level are moderately likely when the market has bullish momentum. Evidence update: Analyst price targets are notably above 285, including a median around 295 and a cited target of 310, which supports upside bias. Alphabet’s earnings/corporate updates emphasize AI, cloud, and monetization strength, which is supportive but only modestly predictive over a single week. Options-implied move around earnings suggests sizable short-term volatility, meaning a move through 285 is feasible, though implied move data cuts both ways. Broader market conditions in late 2025 look constructive for tech, and the company appears to have positive fundamental momentum. Counterweight: analyst targets are longer-horizon and can overstate near-term weekly probability; a single week close is still noisy. Synthesis: I start from a roughly even chance slightly below 1/2 for clearing a specific weekly threshold and move upward due to bullish analyst sentiment, strong fundamentals, and the possibility of post-earnings or momentum-driven continuation. Calibration check: A 0.61 probability means this should happen in a bit over 3 out of 5 similar cases, which feels plausible but not certain.

question:
Will the highest temperature in London be between 54-55°F on October 26?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 26 Oct '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, the London City Airport station’s finalized daily maximum on 26 Oct 2025 must round to 54 or 55°F. That requires a fairly specific late-October temperature outcome, not just a generally mild day. Base rate: In London, late-October daily highs are usually around the low-to-mid 50s °F, but the exact whole-degree maximum landing in the 54-55°F bin is only one small slice of outcomes. Evidence update: The climate and travel references suggest late October in London commonly sees highs near 50-60°F, which makes 54-55°F plausible but not especially likely. The station-specific sources do not provide a direct forecast for that date, and the available forecast snippets point more broadly to temperatures around the mid-to-upper 50s °F for nearby dates, implying 54-55°F is possible but not singled out. Since the market resolves to a whole-degree high, even a small deviation to 53°F or 56°F would fail YES. Synthesis: Starting from a modest base rate for an exact 2-degree bin, the climatology supports a nontrivial chance, but not enough to justify a high probability. Calibration check: If I made 100 such predictions at about 6%, I’d expect roughly 6 YES outcomes, which feels appropriate for an exact-temperature-bin question this far out and with limited station-specific evidence.

question:
Will the highest temperature in London be between 55-56°F on November 7?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 7 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, London City Airport’s highest temperature on 7 Nov 2025 must be reported by Wunderground as 55-56°F, which implies a daily max of about 13-13.3°C rounded to the source’s whole-degree Fahrenheit resolution. Base rate: In early November, London’s typical daily maximum is closer to the upper 40s to low 50s °F, with 55-56°F being plausible but not the modal outcome. Evidence update: The long-run November average maximum for London/Heathrow is around 8.6-11.3°C (47.5-52.3°F), which centers below the target band. The London November weather summary suggests a broad November daytime range of 48-57°F, but that spans the whole month and does not specifically favor the exact 55-56°F bin. London’s climate record also shows November record highs around 62.2°F, indicating the target band is well below record territory but still above average. Taken together, the evidence nudges toward a cool-to-mild day being more likely than an exact 55-56°F top temperature. Synthesis: I’d expect the most likely whole-degree Fahrenheit max on Nov 7 to land near 50-54°F, with 55-56°F a minority outcome. Calibration check: If I made 100 such forecasts at 16%, about 16 should resolve YES; that feels about right for this specific bin.

question:

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, ABNB must trade at or above $158 on at least one regular-hours 1-minute candle high during November 2025. That requires either a meaningful positive catalyst or a broad market/sector surge large enough to push the stock to a new high relative to its then-current range.

Base rate: For a large-cap growth stock to hit a specific upside threshold of roughly 10%-20% above a plausible current level within a single month, the prior is modest but not tiny. Single-month touches of a higher strike/target are more common than month-end closes, but still limited.

Evidence update: The shareholder letter and travel-industry sources point to healthy underlying business conditions, which supports upside. Analyst targets cited are mostly in the low-to-mid $100s, with some bullish targets around $151 and a 2026 estimate around $165, suggesting $158 is not absurdly far away but is above the consensus cluster. Importantly, the available analyst data do not show a strong consensus above $158, and several ratings remain neutral/underweight. That tempers the probability. The company’s strong Q3 and favorable travel-demand backdrop help, but they are not enough on their own to make a $158 intramonth high likely.

Synthesis: Starting from a modest base rate, I move upward somewhat because $158 is within reach if the stock is already near the mid-$140s and gets a favorable earnings or macro move. I do not move much higher because the evidence points to gradual appreciation rather than a sharp breakout, and the analyst target distribution sits mostly below the threshold. Net result: a low-teens probability.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 11.8%, I would expect about 12 YES outcomes, which feels plausible for this kind of one-month touch event.

question:

label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Oliver Bearman to win sprint qualifying pole at the 2025 Brazilian Grand Prix, he would need to outperform the full current F1 field in a short-format qualifying session at Interlagos, likely against multiple established top-tier drivers and cars from stronger teams. Base rate: A rookie/low-probability driver taking sprint qualifying pole in a modern F1 season is rare; at a sprint venue, pole is usually concentrated among the very strongest teams and championship contenders. Bearman’s team and prior sprint-qualifying results suggest he is not a typical pole favorite. Evidence update: The 2025 Sao Paulo weather forecast suggests potentially unsettled conditions, which can increase variance and create upset opportunities, but the forecast still notes likely dry conditions for sprint qualifying and low confidence only modestly increases randomness, not enough to make Bearman a favorite. Haas’s 2025 US GP sprint qualifying recap shows Bearman qualified 16th there, indicating he has not been near sprint pole pace in comparable 2025 sprint qualifying conditions. Historical Interlagos/sprint context shows the venue can produce surprises, and Haas has had a notable pole there in the past, but that was a special-case result and not strong evidence Bearman himself is likely to replicate it. Overall synthesis: Bearman has a nonzero upset chance, especially if weather or red-flag chaos intervenes, but the combination of team strength, driver experience, and historical pole concentration makes a pole position for him very unlikely. Calibration check: If I assigned 0.87%, I would expect about 1 success in 115 such forecasts; that feels consistent with an extremely rare but not impossible outcome.

question:

Synthesis: I move above a pure base rate because credible reports say teams are calling and Hall is on the final year of his rookie deal, but I stay below 50% because being a trade candidate is not the same as being traded, and productive RB trades still fail more often than they happen. question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be between 51-52°F on October 27?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 27 Oct '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, LaGuardia’s finalized daily high on Oct 27, 2025 must be recorded as 51 or 52°F by Wunderground. That requires a fairly narrow outcome range, and the market resolves on a whole-degree daily maximum.

Base rate: Late October in New York City usually has highs in the upper 50s to low 60s, with day-to-day variability. A specific 2-degree bin like 51-52°F is plausible but not the modal outcome. On a given late-October day, a bin this narrow typically has low single-digit to low-teens probability depending on the weather regime.

Evidence update: The strongest evidence points slightly toward cooler-than-normal late October conditions. The AmericanWx discussion explicitly mentions an AO-/NAO-/PNA+ blocking regime and says an extended period of cooler than normal weather will prevail through the end of the month, with temperatures reaching the lower and middle 50s. That increases the chance of a 51-52°F high. The NOAA/Northeast outlook evidence is more neutral/uncertain and does not strongly support either side. Climate normals and general fall outlooks are only weakly informative. The Wunderground page evidence confirms the station and resolution source, but not the actual forecast value. Overall, the evidence nudges upward from a low base rate, but not enough to make 51-52°F the most likely single bin.

Synthesis: Starting from a modest base rate for a 2-degree bin, I increase probability because the regime described is specifically cool and mentions lower/middle 50s, which overlaps the target range. But because the forecast language is broad, the exact bin remains uncertain, and highs just above 52°F or below 51°F remain very plausible. I land at about 5.3%.

Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 5.3%, I would expect about 5 to 6 to resolve YES, which feels reasonable for a narrow temperature bin under a cool-but-not-pinpoint forecast.

question:
Will Israel strike Gaza on November 24?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Gaza soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Gaza ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Gaza Strip counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Gaza territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.
label: YES
rationale: Decompose: A YES requires that on Nov. 24, Israel launches at least one qualifying air/drone/missile strike that impacts Gaza territory, and that this is confirmed by credible reporting within the resolution window. Base rate: In an active Israel-Gaza war period, the daily probability of at least one Israeli aerial strike on Gaza is historically very high, especially absent a durable ceasefire. Evidence update: The strongest evidence is the UN/OCHA flash update stating that a humanitarian pause was agreed to start on 24 November, but it also says Israeli strikes from air, land and sea had reportedly intensified over the previous 24 hours across most of Gaza as of 23 November afternoon. That strongly suggests continued strike activity around the date, though a pause could reduce the chance somewhat on the exact day. The other sources broadly confirm a long pattern of Israeli air operations in Gaza and the ongoing war context, which supports a high base rate but adds little incremental information for this specific date. Synthesis: Because the market only needs one qualifying aerial/drone/missile strike on that date, and because the conflict context plus the OCHA report make continued strikes on or around Nov. 24 more likely than not, I assign a high probability, but not near-certain because a pause/ceasefire could have temporarily suppressed strikes or shifted them outside the date window. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar forecasts in comparable late-war, pause-agreement conditions, about 94 would resolve YES.

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

Synthesis: I start near 2% and make only a small upward adjustment because any live Q&A can introduce unexpected wording. There is no strong evidence that “Skip” is a recurring Walmart term, so the probability remains very low.

question:

Base rate: In modern F1, the fastest lap is usually set by a top-5 car or a driver with a late pit stop for soft tyres. A midfield/low-end driver winning fastest lap is rare, and a specific rookie/young driver in a non-dominant car is rarer still. Qatar is also a sprint weekend with tyre-lap limits and a demanding track, which tends to favor strategic late soft-tyre attempts by stronger cars rather than a lower-midfield driver. Historical Qatar fastest laps in the cited material are from elite drivers like Norris and Verstappen, reinforcing the strong favorite profile. Nothing in the evidence suggests Doohan is unusually likely to have the car pace, race position, or strategy needed. Synthesis: I start from a moderate base rate, then add a meaningful but not overwhelming upward adjustment for Musk’s demonstrated ability to sustain very high volume and the likely event-driven activity around early November 2025. question:
Will turnout be between 52% and 56% in the 2025 Irish Presidential Election?

Description: The Irish Presidential Election is scheduled to be held on October 24, 2025.

This market will resolve based on the total percentage of all votes cast (total poll) out of the number of people entitled to vote (the total electorate) in the 2025 Irish Presidential Election. Spoiled or invalid votes will still count toward total turnout for the purpose of this market.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If voting in the 2025 Irish Presidential Election does not occur by February 28, 2026, this market will resolve to the lowest listed bracket.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Electoral Commission of Ireland (electoralcommission.ie).
label: NO
rationale:
Base rate for Irish presidential election turnout landing in a narrow mid-50s band is modest. Recent presidential turnout has been 43.9% in 2018, and Irish presidential elections often see participation well below general-election levels. For a YES outcome, turnout would need to rise materially from 2018 and land specifically between 52% and 56%, which is a fairly tight interval.

Evidence pushes somewhat toward higher turnout than 2018 because the 2025 race appears competitive and heavily covered, but several indicators cut against a strong turnout surge. The campaign has been described as lacklustre, with concerns that many voters may spoil ballots or abstain. The voter-registration deadlines and standard eligibility rules do not themselves imply unusually high participation. Polling evidence suggests significant undecided/non-committal sentiment, which can reduce certainty that the race will energize turnout. The fact that spoiled ballots count toward turnout slightly helps the market if protest voting rises, but that effect is usually not large enough to move turnout into the mid-50s by itself.

Overall, I’d expect turnout to be more likely in the high 40s to low 50s than in the 52-56% band. If I made 100 such forecasts at 0.214, about 21 would resolve YES, which feels calibrated for this question.

question:
Will the highest temperature in London be between 58-59°F on November 13?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 13 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, London City Airport’s finalized highest temperature on 13 Nov 2025 must land in the 58-59°F bin (roughly 14-15°C). That requires a fairly mild November day, but not an unusually warm one, and the station’s daily high must resolve to whole degrees Fahrenheit in that narrow range.

Base rate: Mid-November highs in London are usually around 50-52°F, with 58-59°F being above normal but not extreme. A one-day high in that bin is plausible, but not common. For a specific date in November at this station, I’d start in the low-teens percent range.

Evidence update: The Met Office trend indicates unsettled weather into November, which usually supports cloud, rain, and cooler daytime highs than a clear sunny spell. The Met Office YouTube forecast says it has been exceptionally mild to start November and may continue for a while, which pushes toward warmer-than-average temperatures. However, the strongest directly relevant climate reference for London/November still centers November averages around about 52-54°F, with 58-59°F above the mean but not impossible. The evidence does not point strongly enough to make 58-59°F the modal outcome, and the unsettled-weather signal slightly outweighs the mild-start signal because the question is about a specific day rather than the month generally.

Synthesis: Combining a modest base rate with mixed evidence yields a low single-digit to high single-digit probability. I land at about 7%, reflecting that 58-59°F is possible but clearly not the most likely bucket.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 7%, about 7 should resolve YES. That feels about right for a narrow temperature bin on a specific November day in London.

Synthesis: Given the repeated polling support and the relatively concentrated competition, I put Person E’s chance of finishing second in the first round at a bit above two-thirds, but not overwhelmingly so because the margin among the contenders is still narrow and late volatility is plausible. The vote count refers to the first final vote on passage in the full chamber, not committee votes or procedural motions

If no such vote is held by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No vote by December 31”.
The resolution sources will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: For a YES, the House must hold a final floor vote on an Epstein disclosure measure by Dec. 31, 2025, and the yea vote count must land between 360 and 369 inclusive. That requires not just passage, but an unusually large bipartisan coalition—roughly 83% to 85% of the 435-member House if full attendance, or a similarly high share of those voting.

Base rate: Final House passage votes on high-salience transparency or investigations measures usually do not cluster in the 360s. They more often fall in the 200s to low 300s, with very broad consensus bills sometimes exceeding 350. So the prior for a specific vote landing in 360-369 is modest.

Evidence update: The reporting indicates the measure has enough support to reach the floor and is expected to pass, with some Republicans willing to break ranks. That pushes toward a high yea count. However, there are also signs of partisan resistance: GOP leadership has opposed the effort, Democrats are whipping in favor but not necessarily unanimous, and the vote is described as having a few centrists potentially breaking ranks. The most important update is that this looks like a likely passage vote, but not necessarily one with near-unanimous support. The existence of a discharge petition and the need for procedural pressure suggests the coalition is broad but still not broad enough to confidently expect 360+ yeas.

Synthesis: Combining a moderate base rate for such an extreme vote total with evidence of substantial but imperfect bipartisan support yields a probability above 50% for the 360-369 band only if the final whip count is exceptionally strong. I still think the more likely outcome is a passage vote with yeas below 360, probably in the low-to-mid 300s. Therefore I assign a somewhat elevated but not dominant chance.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 0.62, about 62 should resolve YES. That feels a bit high for such a narrow vote-count band, so I am slightly cautious, but still near that level because the measure appears to have unusually broad bipartisan support and could attract many Republicans plus most Democrats.

question:

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: A YES requires Trump to make at least one publicly accessible verbal mention of “Cambodia” during Nov 3–9, 2025. Because the term is highly topical and already central to recent diplomacy, the main question is whether he appears in any audio/video setting this week and says it aloud.

Base rate: For a country name that is currently in the news and tied to recent presidential trade activity, the chance of at least one mention in a given week is fairly high, though not certain because it depends on his schedule and whether he speaks publicly.

Evidence update: The evidence strongly increases the probability. Multiple sources show Trump just signed an Agreement on Reciprocal Trade with Cambodia on Oct. 26, 2025, and the White House/USTR were publicly highlighting it on Oct. 26–28. That makes Cambodia a salient talking point likely to recur in remarks, press availabilities, rallies, or interviews. The trade deal is fresh enough that any public discussion of Asia, tariffs, or recent foreign-policy wins could easily include the word. I do not see evidence that would push strongly toward NO, aside from the fact that the market window is only one week and written posts do not count.

Synthesis: Starting from a moderate-high base rate for a salient foreign-policy term, the recency of the Cambodia trade agreement and the likelihood Trump references his recent Asia trip or trade wins push this to a high but not near-certain probability. I would expect roughly 83 of 100 similar weekly forecasts to resolve YES.

Calibration check: 0.83 feels plausible because this is a prominent, recent issue that Trump has incentives to mention, but there remains meaningful uncertainty about whether he will speak publicly and say the specific word aloud during the window.

question:

label: NO
rationale: Lewis Hamilton winning fastest lap at Qatar is possible but unlikely. Decompose: for a YES, Hamilton must be competitive enough on pace, have an in-race strategy or late-race tire advantage that yields the single quickest lap, and not be blocked by faster front-runners or a safety-car-dominated race. Base rate: fastest lap in modern F1 is usually taken by a top car/driver, but it is still distributed across several contenders rather than a single favorite; Hamilton specifically is not the dominant fastest-lap candidate in 2025 given Ferrari/McLaren/Red Bull pace distribution and his team context. Evidence update: Qatar is a high-degradation, strategy-sensitive circuit with a mandatory 25-lap tire cap, which can create late-race opportunities for a driver on fresh tires; that slightly helps Hamilton. However, the strongest recent Qatar-specific evidence points to Max Verstappen and Lando Norris as prior pace benchmarks, and the 2024 Qatar fastest-lap classification showed Hamilton only 9th on fastest-lap timing, not near the top. The FIA/formula1 sources also indicate the race is a 57-lap event with a sprint weekend and strict tire limits, which increases variability but does not specifically advantage Hamilton over the field. Synthesis: Hamilton has some outsider chance via late pit-stop timing or safety-car timing, but absent unusually favorable race circumstances he is not the most likely fastest-lap setter. Calibration check: if I made 100 similar predictions at 2.8%, about 3 should resolve YES, which feels about right for a single-driver fastest-lap bet of this kind.

Synthesis: Combining a moderate base rate with modest positive team-specific signals and the strong presence of other pole threats yields a probability meaningfully below 50%. I would expect Leclerc to take pole in roughly 28 out of 100 similar forecasts.

question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be between 61-62°F on October 20?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 20 Oct '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, LaGuardia’s observed daily high on 2025-10-20 must land exactly in the 61-62°F bucket, which because the source rounds to whole degrees effectively means a reported high of 61 or 62°F. Base rate: In late October in New York City, daily highs around the low 60s are plausible but not common; the climatological mean high is near the upper 60s, and day-to-day weather variability means a specific 2-degree bin on a specific date is usually a low-probability event. Evidence update: The climate normals and monthly records indicate October highs are typically warmer than 61-62°F, with mean maximums around 79.7°F for the month in the cited table, but that table is not directly the daily average high and likely overstates the relevant statistic; still, it supports that 61-62°F is below typical October warmth. The NOAA/NWS LaGuardia historical data source confirms the station and record framework but gives no direct clue for the specific date. The forecast/outlook documents are broad and not date-specific enough to materially increase confidence in 61-62°F. Overall, this is a plausible shoulder-season temperature but still a relatively narrow target range, so the probability remains low. Calibration check: Out of 100 such predictions, I would expect roughly 3 to resolve YES.

label: NO
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, London City Airport’s highest temperature on 1 Dec 2025 must be recorded by Wunderground as exactly 57°F or 58°F, since the market resolves to the whole-degree Fahrenheit bucket containing the day’s maximum.

Base rate: In London in early December, daily highs are usually around the upper 40s to low 50s °F, with 57–58°F being somewhat above typical but not rare. A single-day maximum landing in this narrow 2°F band is a modest-probability event.

Evidence update: The climate references show London’s December mean daily maximum is roughly 47.5–48.0°F, which pushes against 57–58°F being the modal outcome. The airport location and London urban heat island can make temperatures a bit warmer than the city average, but not enough to make 57–58°F especially likely. No forecast-specific evidence strongly supports an unusually warm 1 Dec 2025, so I do not update upward much. The fact that the market uses the airport station also slightly lowers variance relative to central London, but that effect is small.

Synthesis: Starting from a low single-digit-to-mid single-digit chance for any exact 2°F bin around the upper 50s in early December, I adjust slightly upward for London’s occasional mild Atlantic-influenced winter days, but downward because the climatological mean is far below this range. Net result is a low probability, around 6.4%.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 6.4%, I would expect about 6 or 7 YES outcomes. That feels about right.

label: NO

Fastest lap in modern F1 is usually captured by a top team or a driver with a late pit stop and clear track, not a midfield car.

Base rate: For any specific non-favorite driver, the prior probability of taking fastest lap in a given race is low. The 2025 preview material points to unpredictable weather and a sprint weekend, but that mainly increases randomness rather than specifically favoring Gasly. No evidence indicates Alpine is among the top contenders for outright pace in 2025.

Synthesis: I slightly raise the base rate for Interlagos chaos, but not enough to overcome the structural disadvantage of a midfield team. Gasly has a plausible outsider path if conditions are mixed and Alpine targets the point, yet the most likely outcome remains a faster lap from a top car or another driver. My probability is therefore around 6%.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 6.2%, I would expect roughly 6 to 7 to resolve YES, which feels appropriate for this kind of long-shot driver-specific fastest-lap market.

Macro evidence is mixed but not strongly negative; U.S. growth and stock markets have been firm, which is supportive for large-cap tech ([11], [12]). Risks remain meaningful: valuation sensitivity, capital spending, margin pressure, regulatory scrutiny, and macro/tariff concerns could cap upside or trigger volatility ([1]-[3], [5], [10], [16]). The evidence is mostly about medium-term fundamentals rather than the exact week, so I apply only a modest upward adjustment rather than a large one. Synthesis: Starting from a roughly even-to-slightly-positive base rate for a threshold-like weekly outcome, the favorable fundamental backdrop pushes the probability above 0.5, but not dramatically because short-horizon price action is noisy and the sources do not indicate a specific near-term catalyst. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar forecasts with this probability, about 63 should resolve YES; that feels plausible for a strong mega-cap with supportive sentiment but substantial short-term uncertainty.

question:
Will Jaelan Phillips be traded before the 2025 NFL trade deadline

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player is traded to another NFL team by the 2025 NFL trade deadline. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the NFL or the player’s team, with confirmation from a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For YES, Jaelan Phillips would need to remain on the Dolphins long enough to be traded to another NFL team before the 2025 trade deadline, and the trade would need to be officially completed or credibly reported as completed by then. Base rate: Midseason trades of established edge rushers do happen, but they are still relatively uncommon, and trades of players who are both productive and under team control are less frequent than rumors suggest. Evidence update: The strongest signals point toward Phillips being part of Miami’s 2025 plans. Multiple roster/preview sources list him on the Dolphins’ depth chart for 2025 training camp (docs 2, 5, 10), and NFL/team content describes him alongside Bradley Chubb and Chop Robinson as part of the pass-rush trio (doc 1). A Dolphins communications transcript and other coverage suggest he was still with the team in late May 2025 (doc 7). On the other hand, the team’s cap situation and trade-rumor commentary indicate Miami could consider moving veterans if the season goes poorly (docs 8, 12, 14), and Phillips has had injury issues that could make him a trade candidate if the Dolphins pivot to selling. However, these are mostly speculative or conditional and do not outweigh the more concrete roster evidence. Synthesis: I start from a modest base rate and move somewhat toward NO because the available evidence shows Phillips as an active part of Miami’s 2025 edge rotation, with no strong reporting of an imminent trade. Still, the possibility is not tiny because deadline sellers sometimes move notable veterans. Calibration check: A 23.6% YES probability means roughly 24 out of 100 such forecasts would resolve YES, which feels about right given the current evidence and the uncertainty around Miami’s 2025 season trajectory.

question:
Will Shuko Aoyama & Cristina Bucsa win the 2025 WTA Tokyo Women’s Doubles Tournament?

The market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed pair wins the 2025 WTA Tokyo Women’s Doubles Tournament. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this pair to win the tournament per the official rules of the WTA Tokyo tournament, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Shuko Aoyama and Cristina Bucsa to win, they must (1) be in the doubles main draw, (2) remain healthy/eligible through multiple rounds, and (3) defeat the field, which in a WTA 500-level event is usually several strong teams including seeded pairs. Base rate: A specific unseeded or modestly seeded doubles team winning a WTA Tokyo-level event is typically a low-single-digit to low-teens percentage proposition depending on draw strength; a reasonable prior for any given named pair is around 8-12%. Evidence update: The WTA draw materials confirm Cristina Bucsa is in the 2025 Tokyo tournament and that Aoyama/Bucsa are scheduled in doubles, so the pair is indeed a real contender rather than a hypothetical. However, the available evidence does not show them as top seeds or favorites; the doubles field includes established seeded teams such as Perez/Townsend and Muhammad/Schuurs, which lowers their win probability. The fact that the market is asking about a single pair in a competitive draw means the prior should remain modest. Synthesis: Starting near 10% and adjusting slightly downward for the presence of stronger seeded opposition yields about 9.1%. Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 9.1%, I would expect roughly 9 wins, which feels consistent with the uncertainty and the competitive nature of the draw.

question:
Will Benjamin Bonzi & Pierre-Hugues Herbert win the 2025 European Open?

Description: This Polymarket will resolve according to the doubles team that wins the 2025 European Open Men’s Doubles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament per the official rules of the European Open, this market will resolve to “No”.

If, for any reason, the winning team is not declared by December 31, 2025, ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For a YES, Bonzi/Herbert must (1) be in the final doubles draw, (2) stay eligible through the event, and (3) win a small ATP 250 doubles field. Base rate: In ATP 250 doubles events, even seeded teams rarely have better than low-to-mid teens title odds; an unproven pair’s prior is typically around 5% to 12% depending on field strength. Evidence update: The strongest positive signal is that Bonzi and Herbert won Marseille earlier in 2025, showing they can win at this level and on indoor hard courts. That pushes upward. However, the European Open entry list shows a competitive field with multiple established doubles teams and seeds, including Hugo Nys/Édouard Roger-Vasselin, Harrison/King, Arends/Johnson, and others, which lowers any one team’s probability. There is also no evidence here that Bonzi/Herbert are a top seed or dominant season-long pair; Bonzi’s singles ranking and the available tournament previews suggest they are plausible entrants but not clear favorites. Synthesis: I start near the middle of the plausible range for a decent ATP 250 doubles team and adjust modestly upward for their Marseille title, but keep the probability well below 10% because doubles outcomes are volatile and the field looks deep. Calibration check: A probability of 8.5% means about 9 wins in 100 similar forecasts, which feels consistent for a solid but non-favorite team in a competitive ATP 250 doubles event.

question:
Will Sabrina Carpenter say "Domingo" during SNL appearance on October 18?

Description: Sabrina Carpenter is scheduled to join the Saturday Night Live on October 18, 2025. (see: https://www.billboard.com/culture/tv-film/sabrina-carpenter-snl-promos-marcello-hernandez-1236091579/)

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Sabrina Carpenter says the listed term during this appearance. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person's first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about "Joe / Biden 5+ times," a mention of "Joe Biden" will count once).

If this appearance is definitively cancelled by Sabrina Carpenter or Saturday Night Live, or otherwise is not released by October 18, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If the appearance has not aired in its entirety by October 19, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on what has aired up to that point.

Resolution source: The resolution source will be audio/video of the appearance.
label: NO
rationale:
Base rate: In a typical SNL hosting appearance, the chance a specific named recurring character term is spoken is modest, because it requires a deliberate sketch/promo reference rather than a generic conversation. For a term tied to a well-known recurring SNL character and a host with some prior connection to that character, the prior is above 50-50 but still far from certain.

Evidence update: The biggest positive signal is that Sabrina Carpenter has already interacted with Marcello Hernandez in promo material and has a documented prior encounter with the Domingo character, which makes a Domingo reference plausible. Several sources also indicate she is both host and musical guest, increasing the amount of airtime and the number of scripted opportunities for a reference. On the other hand, the evidence does not show any confirmed sketch, promo, or cue-card line containing the word Domingo for the October 18 appearance itself. SNL episodes often include many sketches, but a specific name mention is still contingent on writing choices and cut-for-time decisions. Also, the market counts only this appearance, not surrounding promos or unrelated performances.

Synthesis: The promo history and the character’s salience push probability upward, but absence of direct confirmation keeps it below even odds. My estimate is that if I made 100 comparable forecasts, about 41 would resolve YES.

Calibration check: 0.41 feels plausible rather than aggressive; it reflects meaningful but not dominant likelihood of a Domingo mention during the episode.

question:
Will Trump say "Crime" or "Drug" during the McDonald's Summit on November 17?

Description: Donald Trump is scheduled to participate in an event titled "The President delivers Remarks at McDonald’s Impact Summit" in Washington on November 17, 2025, 6:00PM ET (see https://www.axios.com/2025/11/17/trump-affordability-mcdonalds-summit).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at the specified event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Pluralization/possessive of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about the scheduled event titled "The President delivers Remarks at McDonald’s Impact Summit" on November 17, 2025, 6:00PM ET (https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/topic/calendar/). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by November 17, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: For a YES, Trump must say either exact term (or plural/possessive/compound form) during the live, broadcast remarks at this specific summit. The event topic is affordability/value, but Trump often adds tangential themes, especially crime and drugs.

Base rate: In public political remarks, Trump frequently mentions both crime and drugs, but not in every appearance. For a single speech with a policy/affordability framing, a rough prior for at least one mention of either term is meaningfully above 50%, but far from certain.

Evidence update: The strongest signal is that Trump’s rhetoric has a documented focus on crime, and recent 2025 remarks explicitly include “crime” in his standard talking points. The summit itself is about affordability, which slightly pulls away from crime/drug talk, but Trump commonly broadens to border, crime, and drug themes even in unrelated settings. Drug is also plausible because his administration has recently emphasized drug pricing and drug policy, though that is more about prescription drugs than the standalone term “drug.” The event title and reported focus on value/affordability make a drug mention somewhat less likely than crime, but the combined probability that he says at least one of the two remains fairly high.

Synthesis: I’d put crime at around 0.49 and drug at around 0.34, with overlap, yielding about 0.62 for at least one of them. I’m not more confident because speeches can stay tightly on-message at summit-style events.

Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 0.62, I’d expect roughly 62 to resolve YES, with uncertainty of a few points either way.

label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: For Czechia to win, they need to be at least slightly stronger than their opponent on the day, with no major lineup or situational disadvantage. Because this is a single match resolved in regular time, variance is material; even a better team often wins only in the 45-60% range depending on opponent strength.

Base rate: For a mid-to-upper international side like Czechia, a generic win probability against an unknown but plausible World Cup-qualifier opponent is usually just above 50% if they are favored, or around 40-45% if near-even. Without the opponent explicitly stated, a conservative prior is near 0.50.

Evidence update: Czechia’s recent team metrics are solid but not dominant: WhoScored shows decent overall rating, moderate possession, and respectable scoring output (doc 001). FootyStats gives xG for 1.49 and xG against 1.23, suggesting a modest positive goal differential and a team that is slightly better than average (doc 003). Elo ranks Czechia around 47th with a rating of 1696, which is competitive internationally but not elite (doc 008). The strongest update toward YES comes from the betting-oriented preview stating market odds around -118 / 1.77 for Czechia to win, implying roughly a 54-55% raw win probability before margin (doc 005). That is a meaningful signal, though it may also be partially promotional and could be less reliable than a true market consensus.

Synthesis: Combining a near-even base rate with modestly positive team quality indicators and a mildly favorable market price pushes Czechia slightly above 50%, but not by a lot because the opponent strength and match context are not fully specified. I would not go much higher than the mid-50s.

Calibration check: If I made 100 similar predictions at 54.7%, I would expect about 55 to resolve YES, which feels plausible given the uncertainty here.

question:

rationale: Decompose: For “another coach” to be the next coach fired, at least one NFL head coach must be fired before Feb. 9, 2026, and the first firing must be someone other than the named reference coach in the market. In practice, this is a bet on whether the 2025 season produces any head-coach firing at all, and if so whether it is not the specific coach implied by the market’s alternative. Base rate: NFL coaching changes are common, with recent seasons often seeing multiple firings and at least one midseason dismissal or end-of-season firing. Evidence update: The provided reporting emphasizes the league’s recent pattern of firings, including multiple examples in 2023 and 2024, and notes that head coaches are frequently dismissed during or after seasons. It also highlights several hot-seat candidates entering 2025, which increases the chance that some coach gets fired. However, the evidence is more about general volatility than about a specific imminent firing, so the update is moderate rather than overwhelming. Synthesis: Combining the historical frequency of coach firings with the current hot-seat environment yields a solid but not extreme probability that another coach will be next to be fired. question:
Will Michelle Bachelet come in second in the 1st round of the 2025 Chilean presidential election?

Description: The 2025 Chilean general election is scheduled for 16 November 2025. The President of Chile is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the second-greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Chilean Electoral Service (https://www.servel.cl/).
label: NO
rationale: Decompose: For Michelle Bachelet to come second in the first round, she would need to be an actual first-round candidate in the 2025 Chilean presidential election, remain visible on the ballot through election day, and outperform a large field of active contenders. Base rate: former presidents sometimes re-enter politics, but a successful first-round presidential comeback after years out of office is uncommon; the prior for a named politician who is not clearly an announced candidate to finish exactly second is very low. Evidence update: the strongest update is that Bachelet herself has publicly said she will not be a candidate (YouTube source), which sharply reduces the probability to near-zero because the market is about first-round vote share, not mere popularity. The polling evidence shows the race is currently among Jara, Kast, Matthei, Kaiser, Parisi, and others, with Bachelet absent from the candidate set, reinforcing that she is not in the race. Servel materials confirm the election timing and rules but do not support her candidacy. Synthesis: combining a very low base rate with direct evidence that she is not running yields a very small residual chance only for an unexpected reversal or ballot-related ambiguity, which is still unlikely. Calibration check: if I made 100 similar predictions at 0.6%, I would expect about 0.6 YES outcomes; that feels appropriately cautious given the evidence.

question:
Will "Predator: Badlands" Opening Weekend Box Office be greater than 26m?

Description: This market will resolve according to how much "Predator: Badlands" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (November 7 - November 9) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by November 16, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

Resolution source: https://www.the-numbers.com/
label: YES
rationale:
Decompose: For YES, Predator: Badlands needs a domestic 3-day opening weekend above $26M on The Numbers. That depends on franchise draw, reviews/tracking, competition, and whether the film behaves like a modest breakout rather than a soft franchise entry.

Base rate: For a PG-13 sci-fi/action franchise title from a major studio opening in wide release, a mid-to-high $20M opening is plausible but not assured. The relevant reference class includes franchise reboots/sequels with moderate awareness and mixed historical ceiling.

Evidence update: Boxoffice Pro and Variety both point to opening expectations centered near $25M-$30M, with one forecast explicitly giving a $25M pinpoint and a $20M-$29M range. That places $26M slightly above the midpoint, meaning the market is close to a coin flip but leaning over the threshold. Deadline’s stronger $40M headline appears more optimistic and likely reflects a bullish scenario rather than consensus, so I discount it. The fact that this is Disney/20th Century, in IMAX and premium formats, and that the film is the only major release this weekend supports a decent opening. On the other hand, Predator is not a top-tier mass-audience brand, so there is meaningful downside risk if turnout is only solid rather than strong.

Synthesis: Combining the consensus tracking around $25M-$30M with the film’s franchise/premium-format upside, I put the chance of clearing $26M at a bit above 60%. This is not a strong conviction because the forecast band straddles the threshold and opening-weekend box office is noisy.

Calibration check: If I made 100 predictions at 0.61, I would expect about 61 to resolve YES; that feels about right for this case.
